Compartment Exam - 2019

Code: 65/1/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 29 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:100

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. The question paper consists of 29 questions divided into four sections A, B, C and D. Section A comprises of 4 questions of one mark each, Section B comprises of 8 questions of two marks each, Section C comprises of 11 questions of four marks each and Section D comprises of 6 questions of six marks each.
  3. All questions in Section A are to be answered in one word, one sentence or as per the exact requirement of the question.
  4. There is no overall choice. However, internal choice has been provided in 1 question of Section A, 3 questions of Section B, 3 questions of Section C and 3 questions of Section D. You have to attempt only one of the alternatives in all such questions.
  5. Use of calculators is not permitted. You may ask for logarithmic tables, if required.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 4 carry 1 mark each.
1.

Find the length of the perpendicular drawn from the point P(3, -4, 5) on the z-axis.

 1

OR

Find the vector equation of a plane, which is at a distance of 5 units from the origin and whose normal vector is {tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}.

 1
2.

Find the cofactor of the element a23 of the determinant {tex}\mathrm{}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex}.

 1
3.

Differentiate {tex} \sqrt{\sin \left(e^x\right)} {/tex} with respect to x.

 1
4.

Find the integrating factor of the differential equation {tex} y \frac{d x}{d y}-2 x=y^3 e^{-y} {/tex}.

 1

SECTION B

Question numbers 5 to 12 carry 2 marks each.
5.

Find a unit vector perpendicular to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}, where {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=4 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+8 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}.

 2

OR

If {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\lambda {/tex} 

 2
6.

Given that A and B are two independent events such that P(A) = 0.3 and P(B) = 0.5. Find P(A | B).

 2

OR

A bag contains 3 white and 2 red balls, another bag contains 4 white and 3 red balls. One ball is drawn at random from each bag. Find the probability that the balls drawn are one white and one red.

 2
7.

The probabilities of A, B and C solving a problem independently are {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} respectively. If all the three try to solve the problem independently, find the probability that the problem is solved. 

 2
8.

If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 5 & -3 \\ -3 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}B^{-1}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 0 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1 and hence find {tex}(\mathrm{AB})^{-1}{/tex}

 2
9.

Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{2 x-x^{2}}}{/tex}

 2
10.

Find : {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos ^{2} x(1-\tan x)^{2}} d x{/tex}

 2

OR

Evaluate {tex}\int_{0}^{1} x(1-x)^{n} d x{/tex} 

 2
11.

If f(x) = {tex}\frac{4x + 3}{6x - 4}{/tex}, x {tex}\neq{/tex} {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}, find fof (x).

 2
12.

Form the differential equation representing the family of curves y = a sin (3x - b), where a and b are arbitrary constants.

 2

SECTION C

Question numbers 13 to 23 carry 4 marks each.
13.

Show that the four points A, B, C and D with position vectors {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}, 3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}, 2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}4 \hat{i}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} respectively are coplanar.

 4
14.

Find the vector equation of the line passing through the point (2, 3, -1) and parallel to the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} = 5 and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}} \cdot(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex} = 4.

 4
15.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation x dx - y ey{tex}\sqrt{1+x^2}{/tex} dy = 0, given that y = 1 when x = 0.
OR
Solve the differential equation x cos {tex} \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = y cos {tex} \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)+x{/tex}.

 4

OR
15.

Solve the differential equation {tex} x \cos \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x} {/tex} {tex} =y \cos \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)+x {/tex}.

 4
16.

Let A = N {tex}\times{/tex} N be the set of all ordered pairs of natural numbers and R be the relation on the set A defined by (a, b) R (c, d) iff ad = bc. Show that R is an equivalence relation.

 4

OR

Show that f: R - {2} {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R - {1} defined by f(x) = {tex}\frac{x}{x - 2}{/tex} is one-one. Also, if g: R - {1} {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R - {2} is defined as g(x) = {tex}\frac{2x}{x - 1}{/tex}, find gof (x).

 4
17.

Using properties of determinants, show that {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 1 & \mathbf{x} & \mathbf{x}^{2} \\ 1 & \mathbf{y} & \mathbf{y}^{2} \\ 1 & \mathbf{z} & \mathbf{z}^{2} \end{array}\right|=(\mathbf{x}-y)(\mathbf{y}-z)(z-x){/tex}

 4
18.

Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}, \text { if } x^{y} \cdot y^{x}=x^{x}{/tex}.

 4

OR

If x = a sec3 {tex}\theta{/tex} and y = a tan3 {tex}\theta{/tex}, find {tex}\frac{\text d^{2} \text y}{\text {dx}^{2}}{/tex}.

 4
19.

Find the equation of the tangent to the curve ay2 = x3 at the point (am2 , am3)

 4
20.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex}

 4
21.

Prove that: {tex}\tan ^ { - 1 } \sqrt { x } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 - x } { 1 + x } \right) , x \in ( 0,1 ).{/tex}

 4
22.

If y = sin-1 x - cos-1 x, show that {tex} \left(1-x^2\right) \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-x \frac{d y}{d x} {/tex} = 0.

 4
23.

Evaluate: {tex} \int \limits_3^5\{|x-3|+|x-4|+|x-5|\} d x {/tex}

 4

SECTION D

Question numbers 24 to 29 carry 6 marks each.
 
24.

A small firm manufactures chairs and tables. Market demand and available resources indicate that the combined production of chairs and tables should not exceed 50 units per day. It takes 30 minutes to manufactures a chair and 1 hour to manufacture a table. A maximum of 40 man-hours per day are available. The profit on each of chairs is ₹ 40 and profit on each table is ₹ 60. Determine how many each chair and tables should be manufactured per day in order to maximize the profit. What is the maximum profit?  Formulate LPP and solve graphically.

 6
25.

{(x, y) : 9x2 + 4y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 36, 3x + 2y {tex}\geq{/tex} 6}
Using integration, find the area of the region enclosed between the two circles x2 + y2 = 1 and (x - 1)2 + y2 = 1.

 6

OR

Using integration, find the area of the region:
{(x, y) : 9x2 + 4y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 36, 3x + 2y {tex}\geq{/tex} 6}

 6
26.

Find the coordinates of the point where the line {tex}\frac{x-8}{4}=\frac{y-1}{1}=\frac{z-3}{8}{/tex} intersects the plane 2x + 2y + z = 3. Also find the angle between the line and the plane.

 6

OR

A line with direction ratios < 2, 2, 1 > intersects the lines {tex}\frac{x-7}{3}=\frac{y-5}{2}=\frac{z-3}{1} \text { and } \frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y+1}{4}=\frac{z+1}{3}{/tex} at the points P and Q respectively. Find the length and the equation of the intercept PQ.

 6
27.

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1. Using A-1, solve the system of equations y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9

 6

OR

Obtain the inverse of the following matrix using elementary operations: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 

 6
28.

Bag I contains 3 white and 4 black balls, while Bag II contains 5 white and 3 black balls. One ball is transferred at random from Bag I to Bag II and then a ball is drawn at random from Bag II. The ball so drawn is found to be white. Find the probability that the transferred ball is also white.

 6
29.

Show that the right circular cylinder of given surface and maximum volume is such that its height is equal to the diameter of the base.

 6

answer_key_html:
Compartment Exam - 2019

Code: 65/1/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 29 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:100

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. The question paper consists of 29 questions divided into four sections A, B, C and D. Section A comprises of 4 questions of one mark each, Section B comprises of 8 questions of two marks each, Section C comprises of 11 questions of four marks each and Section D comprises of 6 questions of six marks each.
  3. All questions in Section A are to be answered in one word, one sentence or as per the exact requirement of the question.
  4. There is no overall choice. However, internal choice has been provided in 1 question of Section A, 3 questions of Section B, 3 questions of Section C and 3 questions of Section D. You have to attempt only one of the alternatives in all such questions.
  5. Use of calculators is not permitted. You may ask for logarithmic tables, if required.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 4 carry 1 mark each.
1.

Find the length of the perpendicular drawn from the point P(3, -4, 5) on the z-axis.

Solution:

Required length = {tex}\sqrt{3^{2}+(-4)^{2}}{/tex}
= 5

 1

OR

Find the vector equation of a plane, which is at a distance of 5 units from the origin and whose normal vector is {tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}.

Solution:

The plane is perpendicular to {tex}\vec r{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} 
The normal vector {tex}\vec n{/tex} to the plane is
{tex}\vec n{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  unit vector along this normal is
{tex}\vec n{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\vec{n}}{|\vec{n}|}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{2^{2}+(-1)^{2}+2^{2}}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}}{3}{/tex} 
The vector equation of the plane in norml form is {tex}\vec r{/tex} = {tex}\vec n{/tex} = p where p is the distance of the plane from the origin. Here p = 5.
{tex}\vec r{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}}{3}{/tex} = 5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\vec r{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} = 15,which is the required vector equation of plane.

 1
2.

Find the cofactor of the element a23 of the determinant {tex}\mathrm{}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex}.

Solution:

Here, A =  {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
First we will find minor of second row.
M21 = 9 - 16 = - 7
M22 = 15 - 8 = 7
M23 = 10 - 3 = 7
Now, we will find the cofactors using these minors.
A21 = ( - 1)2+1 M21 = - 1( - 7) = 7
A22 = ( - 1)2+2 M22 = 1(7) = 7
A23 = ( - 1)2+3 M23 = - 1(7) = - 7
Now, a21 = 2
a22 = 0
a23 = 1
Therefore value of  {tex}\Delta{/tex}  = a21A21 + a22A22 + a23A23 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 2(7) + 0(7) + 1(−7) = 14 + 0 − 7 = 7
Thus we have  {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 7.

 1
3.

Differentiate {tex} \sqrt{\sin \left(e^x\right)} {/tex} with respect to x.

Solution:

{tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{e^x \cdot \cos \left(e^x\right)}{2 \sqrt{\sin \left(e^x\right)}} {/tex}

 1
4.

Find the integrating factor of the differential equation {tex} y \frac{d x}{d y}-2 x=y^3 e^{-y} {/tex}.

Solution:

{tex} \frac{d x}{d y}-\frac{2}{y} \cdot x=y^2 e^{-y} {/tex}
I.F. {tex}=\mathrm{e}^{\int-\frac{2}{y} d y}=e^{-2 \log y}=\frac{1}{y^2} {/tex}

 1

SECTION B

Question numbers 5 to 12 carry 2 marks each.
5.

Find a unit vector perpendicular to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}, where {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=4 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+8 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}.

Solution:

Here we have {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=4 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+8 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{{a}} \times \overrightarrow{{b}}{/tex}={tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 4 & -1 & 8 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right|{/tex}= {tex}7 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}{/tex}
Required unit vector = {tex}\frac{(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})}{|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{9}(7 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}){/tex}

 2

OR

If {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\lambda {/tex} 

Solution:

Given: {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} 

Now {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k + \lambda \left( { - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k} \right){/tex}=

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cb = da

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a, b) R(e, f) {tex}\therefore{/tex} R is transitive

Equation of line : {tex}\frac{x- 8}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{y - 1}{1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{z-3}{8}{/tex} 
Equation of plane : 2x + 2y + z = 3
Let {tex}\frac{x- 8}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{y - 1}{1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{z-3}{8}{/tex} = k
Substitute values of x, y and z in equation of plane
2(4k + 8) + 2(k + 1) + 8k + 3 = 3
18k = -18
k = -1
x = -4 + 8 = 4, y = -1 + 1 = 0, z = -8 + 3 = 5
Point of intersection (4, 0, 5)
Angle between line and plane is given by formula;
sin {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}|\frac{n \cdot b}{In |b|}|{/tex} 
Here, n = (2, 2, 1) and b = (4, 1, 8)
sin {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \cdot 4 + 2 \cdot 1 + 1 \cdot 8}{\sqrt{4^2 + 1^2+8^2} \times \sqrt{2^2+2^2+ 1^2}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{18}{3 \times \sqrt{71}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{\sqrt{71}}{/tex} 
{tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{\sqrt{71}}{/tex}

A line with direction ratios < 2, 2, 1 > intersects the lines {tex}\frac{x-7}{3}=\frac{y-5}{2}=\frac{z-3}{1} \text { and } \frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y+1}{4}=\frac{z+1}{3}{/tex} at the points P and Q respectively. Find the length and the equation of the intercept PQ.

Solution:


Let P(3{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 7, 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 5, {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 3) and
Q(2{tex}\mu{/tex} + 1, 4{tex}\mu{/tex} - 1, 3{tex}\mu{/tex} - 1)
Now, d.r′.s. of PQ = 3{tex}\lambda{/tex} - 2{tex}\mu{/tex} + 6, 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} - 4{tex}\mu{/tex} + 6, {tex}\lambda{/tex} - 3{tex}\mu{/tex} + 4
According to question,
{tex}\frac{3 \lambda-2 \mu+6}{2}{/tex}={tex}\frac{2 \lambda-4 \mu+6}{2}{/tex}={tex}\frac{\lambda-3 \mu+4}{1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 2{tex}\mu{/tex} = 0 and 2{tex}\mu{/tex} = 2 ⇒ {tex}\mu{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = -2{tex}\mu{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex} P(1, 1, 1) and Q(3, 3, 2)
PQ = {tex}\sqrt{(3-1)^{2}+(3-1)^{2}+(2-1)^{2}}{/tex}

27.

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1. Using A-1, solve the system of equations y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9

Solution:

{tex}|A|{/tex}= {tex}\begin{vmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = -1(1-9)+2(1-6) = 8-10 

{tex}|A|{/tex} = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 exists
Now, A11 = -1, A12 = 8, A13 = -5
A21 = 1, A22 = -6, A23 = 3
A31 = -1 , A32 = 2, A33 = -1
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex}adjA = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given system of equations are

y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9

It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B,
where A ={tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now AX = B ⇒ X = A-1B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{l}-4 \\ -2 \\ -4\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}2 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2

28.

Bag I contains 3 white and 4 black balls, while Bag II contains 5 white and 3 black balls. One ball is transferred at random from Bag I to Bag II and then a ball is drawn at random from Bag II. The ball so drawn is found to be white. Find the probability that the transferred ball is also white.

Solution:

Let E1: Transferred ball is white
E2: Transferred ball is black
A: white ball is found
Here, {tex} \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_1\right)=\frac{3}{7}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_2\right)=\frac{4}{7} {/tex}
{tex} \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A} / \mathrm{E}_1\right)=\frac{6}{9}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A} / \mathrm{E}_2\right)=\frac{5}{9} {/tex}
Using Baye’s theorem
{tex} P\left(E_1 / A\right)=\frac{P\left(E_1\right) \cdot P\left(A / E_1\right)}{P\left(E_1\right) \cdot P\left(A / E_1\right)+P\left(E_2\right) \cdot P\left(A / E_2\right)} {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{\frac{3}{7} \times \frac{6}{9}}{\frac{3}{7} \times \frac{6}{9}+\frac{4}{7} \times \frac{5}{9}} {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{18}{18+20}=\frac{9}{19} {/tex}

29.

Show that the right circular cylinder of given surface and maximum volume is such that its height is equal to the diameter of the base.

Solution:

Let radius of the cylinder = r
Height of the cylinder = h|

{tex}s = 2\pi rh + 2\pi {r^2}{/tex}     (1)
{tex}\Rightarrow\frac{{s-2\pi {r^2}}}{{2\pi r}} = h{/tex} 
Now volume of cylinder is, {tex}v = \pi {r^2}h{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}v = \pi .{r^2}\left( {\frac{{s - 2\pi {r^2}}}{{2\pi r}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow v = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {sr - 2\pi {r^3}} \right]{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\frac{{dv}}{{dr}} = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {s - 6\pi {r^2}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow\frac{{{d^2}v}}{{d{r^2}}} = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {0 - 12\pi r} \right]{/tex} 
For maximum/minimum
{tex}\frac{{dv}}{{dr}} = 0{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow s = 6\pi {r^2}{/tex} 
From equ (1)
{tex}\Rightarrow 2\pi rh + \ 2{tex}\pi{/tex} {r^2} = 6\pi {r^2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow r = \frac{h}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}{\left[ {\frac{{{d^2}v}}{{d{r^2}}}} \right]_{r = \frac{h}{2}}} = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {0 - 12\pi \times \frac{h}{2}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex} = - 3\pi h < 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} s is maximum at {tex}r = \frac{h}{2}{/tex} 
Hence h = 2r

SECTION A Question numbers 1 to 4 carry 1 mark each. 1. Find the length of the perpendicular drawn from the point P(3, -4, 5) on the z-axis. Solution: Required length = {tex}\sqrt{3^{2}+(-4)^{2}}{/tex} = 5 1 OR Find the vector equation of a plane, which is at a distance of 5 units from the origin and whose normal vector is {tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} . Solution: The plane is perpendicular to {tex}\vec r{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} The normal vector {tex}\vec n{/tex} to the plane is {tex}\vec n{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} unit vector along this normal is {tex}\vec n{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\vec{n}}{|\vec{n}|}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{2^{2}+(-1)^{2}+2^{2}}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}}{3}{/tex} The vector equation of the plane in norml form is {tex}\vec r{/tex} = {tex}\vec n{/tex} = p where p is the distance of the plane from the origin. Here p = 5. {tex}\vec r{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}}{3}{/tex} = 5 {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\vec r{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} = 15,which is the required vector equation of plane. 1 2. Find the cofactor of the element a 23 of the determinant {tex}\mathrm{}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} . Solution: Here, A = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} First we will find minor of second row. M 21 = 9 - 16 = - 7 M 22 = 15 - 8 = 7 M 23 = 10 - 3 = 7 Now, we will find the cofactors using these minors. A 21 = ( - 1) 2+1 M 21 = - 1( - 7) = 7 A 22 = ( - 1) 2+2 M 22 = 1(7) = 7 A 23 = ( - 1) 2+3 M 23 = - 1(7) = - 7 Now, a 21 = 2 a 22 = 0 a 23 = 1 Therefore value of {tex}\Delta{/tex} = a 21 A 21 + a 22 A 22 + a 23 A 23 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 2(7) + 0(7) + 1(−7) = 14 + 0 − 7 = 7 Thus we have {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 7. 1 3. Differentiate {tex} \sqrt{\sin \left(e^x\right)} {/tex} with respect to x. Solution: {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{e^x \cdot \cos \left(e^x\right)}{2 \sqrt{\sin \left(e^x\right)}} {/tex} 1 4. Find the integrating factor of the differential equation {tex} y \frac{d x}{d y}-2 x=y^3 e^{-y} {/tex} . Solution: {tex} \frac{d x}{d y}-\frac{2}{y} \cdot x=y^2 e^{-y} {/tex} I.F. {tex}=\mathrm{e}^{\int-\frac{2}{y} d y}=e^{-2 \log y}=\frac{1}{y^2} {/tex} 1 SECTION B Question numbers 5 to 12 carry 2 marks each. 5. Find a unit vector perpendicular to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} , where {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=4 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+8 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} . Solution: Here we have {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=4 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+8 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{{a}} \times \overrightarrow{{b}}{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 4 & -1 & 8 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}7 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}{/tex} Required unit vector = {tex}\frac{(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})}{|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{9}(7 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} 2 OR If {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}\lambda {/tex} Solution: Given: {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} Now {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k + \lambda \left( { - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k} \right){/tex} = {tex} = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k - \lambda \hat i + 2\lambda \hat j + \lambda \hat k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec a + \lambda \vec b = \left( {2 - \lambda } \right)\hat i + \left( {2 + 2\lambda } \right)\hat j + \left( {3 + \lambda } \right)\hat k{/tex} Again, {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j = 3\hat i + \hat j + 0\hat k{/tex} Since, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} therefore, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right).\vec c = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6 - 3\lambda + 2 + 2\lambda = 0 \Rightarrow - \lambda + 8 = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow - \lambda = - 8 \Rightarrow \lambda = 8{/tex} 2 6. Given that A and B are two independent events such that P(A) = 0.3 and P(B) = 0.5. Find P(A | B). Solution: 0.3 Explanation: P(A/B) = {tex}\frac{P(A \cap B)}{P(B)}=\frac{P(A) P(B)}{P(B)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{0.5×0.3}{0.5}=0.3{/tex} 2 OR A bag contains 3 white and 2 red balls, another bag contains 4 white and 3 red balls. One ball is drawn at random from each bag. Find the probability that the balls drawn are one white and one red. Solution: Required probability = {tex}\frac{3}{5} \times \frac{3}{7}+\frac{2}{5} \times \frac{4}{7}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{17}{35}{/tex} 2 7. The probabilities of A, B and C solving a problem independently are {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} , {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} respectively. If all the three try to solve the problem independently, find the probability that the problem is solved. Solution: Required probability = 1 - P(problem is not solved) = 1 - {tex}{P}\left({A}^{\prime} \cap {B}^{\prime} \cap {C}^{\prime}\right){/tex} = 1 - P(A') P(B') P(C') = 1 - {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{3}{4}=\frac{3}{4}{/tex} 2 8. If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 5 & -3 \\ -3 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}B^{-1}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 0 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 and hence find {tex}(\mathrm{AB})^{-1}{/tex} Solution: We have |A| = 10 - 9 = 1 also c 11 = 2 , c 12 = 3, c 21 = 3, c 22 = 5, Therefore , A -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 3 \\ 3 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} Also , (AB) -1 = B -1 A -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr}3 & 2 \\ 0 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 3 \\ 3 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}12 & 19 \\ -3 & -5\end{array}\right]{/tex} 2 9. Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{2 x-x^{2}}}{/tex} Solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{2 x-x^{2}}}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{1-(x-1)^{2}}}{/tex} Put x - 1 = t. Then dx = dt. Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{2 x-x^{2}}}=\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{1-t^{2}}}=\sin ^{-1}(t)+C{/tex} = sin -1 (x-1) + C 2 10. Find : {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos ^{2} x(1-\tan x)^{2}} d x{/tex} Solution: The given integral is: I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos ^{2} x(1-\tan x)^{2}} d x=\int \frac{sec^2x}{(1-tanx)^2}dx{/tex} Let {tex}1-tanx=z~ \Rightarrow~ -sec^2xdx=dz{/tex} {tex}\therefore ~ I = -\int\frac 1{z^2}dz\\=-\frac {z^{-2+1}}{-2+1}+c \\ =\frac1z+c\\=\frac1{1-tanx}+c{/tex} 2 OR Evaluate {tex}\int_{0}^{1} x(1-x)^{n} d x{/tex} Solution: {tex}\text { Let } I=\int_{0}^{1} x(1-x)^{n} d x \text { . Then, }{/tex} we have {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1}(1-x)\left\{1-\left.(1-x)\right|^{n} d x\right.{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\int_{0}^{1}(1-x) x^{n} d x=\int_{0}^{1}\left(x^{n}-x^{n+1}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\left[\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}-\frac{x^{n+2}}{n+2}\right]_{0}^{1}=\left\{\frac{1}{n+1}-\frac{1}{n+2}\right\}-(0-0)=\frac{1}{(n+1)(n+2)}{/tex} 2 11. If f(x) = {tex}\frac{4x + 3}{6x - 4}{/tex} , x {tex}\neq{/tex} {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} , find fof (x). Solution: fof(x) = f(f(x)) {tex} =f\left(\frac{4 x+3}{6 x-4}\right) {/tex} {tex} =\frac{4\left(\frac{4 x+3}{6 x-4}\right)+3}{6\left(\frac{4 x+3}{6 x-4}\right)-4}=\frac{34 x}{34}=x {/tex} 2 12. Form the differential equation representing the family of curves y = a sin (3x - b), where a and b are arbitrary constants. Solution: y = a sin (3x - b) {tex} \frac{d y}{d x} {/tex} = 3a cos (3x - b) {tex} \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2} {/tex} = -9a sin (3x - b) {tex} \Rightarrow\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=-9 y {/tex} or {tex} \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}+9 y {/tex} = 0 2 SECTION C Question numbers 13 to 23 carry 4 marks each. 13. Show that the four points A, B, C and D with position vectors {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}, 3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}, 2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}4 \hat{i}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} respectively are coplanar. Solution: We need to show that the four points corresponding to four given position vectors {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}, 3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}, 2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}4 \hat{i}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} respectively are coplanar. Here we have, A(1, 2, -1), B(3, -1, 0), C(2, 3, 2), D(4, 0, 3) Now, {tex}\overrightarrow{{AB}}=2 \hat{{i}}-3 \hat{{j}}+\hat{{k}}{/tex} , {tex}\overrightarrow{{AC}}=\hat{{i}}+\hat{{j}}+3 \hat{{k}}{/tex} , {tex}\overrightarrow{{AD}}=3 \hat{{i}}-2 \hat{{j}}+4 \hat{{k}}{/tex} So, {tex}[\overrightarrow{{AB}} \overrightarrow{{AC}} \overrightarrow{{AD}}]{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rrr}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 & 3 \\ 3 & -2 & 4\end{array}\right|{/tex} = 2(10) +3(-5) +1(-5) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A, B, C and D are coplanar 4 14. Find the vector equation of the line passing through the point (2, 3, -1) and parallel to the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} = 5 and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}} \cdot(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex} = 4.
